name: Festivals & Holidays
description: World festivals, patron saints, and holidays through the year

questions.q: St. Patrick is the patron saint of which country?

source.notes: Feast day March 17.

questions.q: St. George is the patron saint of which country?

source.notes: St. George's Day is April 23.

questions.q: St. Andrew is the patron saint of which country?

source.notes: Feast day November 30; the saltire (diagonal cross) features on the Scottish flag.

questions.q: France's national holiday Bastille Day is celebrated on which date?

source.notes: Commemorates the storming of the Bastille prison on July 14, 1789.

questions.q: Burns Night, celebrated January 25, honors the national poet of which country?

source.notes: Marks Robert Burns's birthday; traditionally includes haggis and whisky.

questions.q: The Glastonbury Festival of Contemporary Performing Arts is held in which country?

source.notes: Held on Worthy Farm in Somerset, England, most years since 1970.

questions.q: The Cannes Film Festival takes place each May in which country?

source.notes: Held on the French Riviera since 1946.

questions.q: Oktoberfest is the world's largest beer festival, held each year in which German city?

source.notes: Held at Theresienwiese in Munich, Bavaria since 1810.

questions.q: The annual Running of the Bulls during the San Fermín festival takes place in which Spanish city?

source.notes: Held each July 6–14; made famous by Hemingway's 'The Sun Also Rises'.

questions.q: The chaotic tomato-throwing festival 'La Tomatina' is held in which country?

source.notes: Held in Buñol, Valencia, each last Wednesday of August.

questions.q: In which London neighbourhood is the UK's biggest street carnival held every August Bank Holiday weekend?

source.notes: Celebrates Caribbean culture; one of the world's largest street festivals.

questions.q: Earth Day was first celebrated on which date in 1970?

source.notes: Founded by U.S. Senator Gaylord Nelson; now marked in 192 countries.

questions.q: Every summer solstice on June 21, crowds gather to watch the sunrise at which prehistoric British monument?

source.notes: Located in Wiltshire, England.

questions.q: The Jewish Festival of Lights, Hanukkah, lasts how many nights?

source.notes: Commemorates the rededication of the Second Temple; one candle per night on the menorah.

questions.q: Mardi Gras is most famously celebrated in which U.S. city?

source.notes: Falls on Shrove Tuesday before Lent; also called 'Fat Tuesday'.

questions.q: German Unity Day (Tag der Deutschen Einheit) is a national holiday on which date?

source.notes: Marks German reunification in 1990.

questions.q: The Carnival of Rio de Janeiro is traditionally held in the period leading up to which Christian season?

source.notes: Six days ending on Ash Wednesday.

questions.q: Europe's biggest traditional Welsh cultural festival, featuring music, poetry, and dance each August, is called what?

source.notes: The National Eisteddfod of Wales moves around the country each year.

questions.q: The Sundance Film Festival is held each January in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Based in Park City, Utah; founded by Robert Redford in 1978.

questions.q: The Edinburgh International Festival of the performing arts was founded in what year?

source.notes: Launched just after WWII; the Fringe started alongside it that same year.

questions.q: Beltane, the ancient Gaelic fire festival, is traditionally celebrated on which date?

source.notes: Marks the halfway point between the spring equinox and the summer solstice.

questions.q: The famous 'Battle of the Wine' (Batalla del Vino) tomato-style wine fight is held every June in which Spanish town?

source.notes: Haro is in La Rioja wine region; festival coincides with San Pedro's Day on June 29.
